2606.28600.title: Neuromorphic Energy-Aware Learning for Adaptive Deep Brain Stimulation
2606.28600.paper_summary: Neuromorphic and edge computing research has focused on reducing the inference cost of neural network controllers, yet in physical closed-loop systems the actuator can rival or exceed an efficient controller in energy. An efficient controller is therefore necessary but not sufficient, because the actuator becomes the cost worth reducing once inference no longer dominates it. Here, we introduce energy-aware learning, an approach that incorporates actuator energy directly into the reinforcement learning reward, and demonstrate it in closed-loop deep brain stimulation (DBS) for Parkinson's disease. A deep spiking Q-network, trained in a biophysical cortico-basal ganglia-thalamic circuit model, learns to suppress pathological alpha-beta oscillations by 45.2% while reducing stimulation charge by 80.0% relative to continuous DBS. Sparsity-constrained knowledge distillation compresses the policy onto the SynSense XyloAudio 3 neuromorphic processor at 0.52 mW inference power, yielding 28.1x lower energy per inference than an equivalent artificial neural network on conventional edge hardware. By co-optimizing stimulation energy and inference efficiency, the framework addresses both major power demands in implantable neuromodulation.
2606.28600.paper_summary_zh: 神經形態和邊緣計算研究專注於降低神經網絡控制器的推斷成本，然而在物理閉環系統中，執行器的能量消耗可以與高效控制器相當或超過。因此，高效控制器是必要的，但不足夠，因為一旦推斷不再主導，執行器就成為值得減少的成本。在這裡，我們介紹了能量感知學習，這是一種將執行器能量直接納入強化學習獎勵的方法，並在針對帕金森病的閉環深腦刺激（DBS）中進行了演示。一個在生物物理皮層-基底神經節-丘腦電路模型中訓練的深度脈衝Q網絡，學會了將病理性α-β振盪抑制45.2%，同時相對於連續DBS減少80.0%的刺激電荷。稀疏約束的知識蒸餾將策略壓縮到SynSense XyloAudio 3神經形態處理器上，推斷功率為0.52 mW，實現每次推斷的能量比在傳統邊緣硬件上的相應人工神經網絡低28.1倍。通過共同優化刺激能量和推斷效率，該框架解決了可植入神經調節中的兩個主要功率需求。
2606.28600.author: Binh Nguyen et.al.
2606.28600.authors: Binh Nguyen, Colleen Josephson, Mircea Teodorescu, Gert Cauwenberghs, Jason Eshraghian
